action: Python in excel no longer working, even though it worked yesterday.  Issue Summary Python in Excel returns #BLOCKED! for a specific user across: •	Excel Desktop and •	Excel for the Web  What has been conclusively ruled out •	Workbook or file trust •	Desktop Excel build/channel •	Network, proxy, VPN, or firewall •	Trust Center / privacy settings •	Calculation mode •	License SKU and activation model •	Python quota exhaustion (no diagnostic message shown)  Key Diagnostic Result Python in Excel is also blocked in Excel for the Web, which confirms the issue is account level or tenant level, not device level.  Most Likely Root Cause Python in Excel is disabled or not entitled for this user via: •	Tenant feature policy •	AI / connected experiences governance •	Backend feature flag misalignment  Required Action M365 Admin / Microsoft Support must: 1.	Verify Python in Excel is enabled for the tenant 2.	Verify the affected user is entitled to Python in Excel 3.	Refresh or re provision Python in Excel entitlements for the user 4.	Confirm no tenant or conditional access policy is blocking Python compute Reinstalling Office or adjusting Trust Center settings will not resolve this. Python in Excel eligibility is not determined solely by license SKU. It is enforced by backend entitlement and feature flags, which must be validated by M365 Admin or Microsoft Support.

Hello Dan,

The freeze applies only to member movement (prov assoc), it does not apply to measure gap results (denom/num). All the members became eligible (per Tech Sepcs) for SPC based on the second IVD diagnosis that occurred late in the year of 2025. One member is excluded which will be reflected at the end of RO, if not earlier:

Tech Specs
 
Please let me know if you have any questions. I will now place the ticket in pending closed.

Thank You
Abdul
rationale: Denominator change during run-out is a quality-measure processing issue typical of HEDIS/quality programs.
